summary: Joel announces the launch of Web Applications Stack Exchange, a new Q&A site for web app questions like exporting Gmail or deleting Facebook accounts. It's the first site created through the community-driven Area 51 process, where proposed sites must demonstrate they can reach critical mass before being approved. The post is essentially a brief promotional announcement for the expanding Stack Exchange network.
key_points: Web Applications Stack Exchange has entered beta as a Q&A site for consumer web app questions
It is the first site created through Area 51, Stack Exchange's community-driven site creation process
New Stack Exchange sites must demonstrate they can reach critical mass before being officially created
money_quotes: It's a part of the Stack Exchange network, so it has the clean, elegant design that made Stack Overflow a phenomenal success.
There are more great sites in the pipeline, but they have to demonstrate that they can reach critical mass or we won't create them.
key_insight: Stack Exchange's growth strategy required new community sites to prove demand through a structured proposal process before being created.
